past_challenges.raw: I spent my first two years as a pre-med student before realizing I didn't actually want to be a doctor — I just thought I should. Switching to Data Science felt terrifying because I worried about what my parents would think and whether I had wasted two years.

After switching majors I had to take a bunch of catch-up classes while my friends were already in upper-division coursework. I felt behind and stressed all the time, but I kept reminding myself that I was finally studying something I actually cared about.
